Fast Retailing Shares Jump After Earnings Beat Expectations
data.description:
By Ronnie Harui


Fast Retailing's shares rose sharply on Friday after the Uniqlo owner reported stronger-than-expected annual results, driven by solid performances as its Japan and international units.

The stock was recently 6.3% higher at Y51,350.00 after climbing as much as 8.1% to Y52,190 yen, its highest intraday level since Jan. 9, 2025.

"The results underscored the firm's strong, stable earnings and operational know-how, so this should ease investors' jitters," said SMBC Nikko Securities' analyst Kuni Kanamori in a research note.

Uniqlo Japan's gross profit margin narrowed only slightly to 50.7% for the fiscal year ended August from 50.8% a year earlier. In North America, business profit margin widened by 1.2 percentage points to 16.3%, even as U.S. tariffs began to take effect.

While the market had been concerned about margin pressure at Uniqlo Japan and the potential impact of U.S. tariffs, the latest results showed positive signs, Kanamori said. Fast Retailing's guidance should also help dispel these concerns, he added.

For the current fiscal year that began in September, Fast Retailing projected revenue to rise 10% to Y3.750 trillion, operating profit to grow 8.1% to Y610.00 billion and net profit to edge up 0.5% to Y435.00 billion.


Write to Ronnie Harui at ronnie.harui@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-10-25 0054ET

Coinbase, Mastercard Reportedly Held Advanced Talks to Buy BVNK
data.description:
Coinbase Global, Inc. (NasdaqGS:COIN) and Mastercard Incorporated (NYSE:MA) have each held advanced acquisition talks to buy London-based BVNK (BVNK Digital Assets Limited), according to six sources familiar with the dealings, who asked for anonymity to talk about confidential business discussions. The terms and winning bidder haven?t been finalized, but the sale price is in the range of $1.5 billion to $2.5 billion, according to some of the sources. The talks may not result in a final deal, but at present Coinbase appears to have the inside track over Mastercard, three of the sources told Fortune.

If any deal is reached, it would be the largest stablecoin acquisition yet, and another signal that stablecoins, or cryptocurrencies pegged to underlying assets like the U.S. dollar, have reached the financial mainstream. And if BVNK decides to sell itself to Mastercard, it?s the clearest sign yet that the incumbent payments network?whose shares fell in June on news that Amazon and Walmart were pursuing stablecoins?is taking the rise of the technology seriously. BVNK, Mastercard, and Coinbase declined to comment.

data.title: Lovaglio: "Generali is Strategic, MPS Focused on Mediobanca"
data.description:
(Alliance News) - MPS CEO Luigi Lovaglio, speaking before the Senate Banking Commission, reiterated the importance of Generali, describing it as "a strategic and profitable asset" not tied to banking activities, according to La Repubblica on Friday.

Lovaglio explained that the 13.2% stake in the insurer offers opportunities in bancassurance, especially with the renegotiation of the agreement with AXA scheduled for 2026.

Following the OPAS on Mediobanca, which saw 86% participation, Lovaglio expressed confidence in the operation, which is supported by Delfin and Caltagirone. "Seventy percent of the capital is held by Italian and foreign funds: ours is a market operation," he clarified.

"The Mediobanca brand is sacred, just like that of MPS," he said, ruling out any departures of managers. On Banco BPM: "It is an excellent bank, but today we are focused on Mediobanca, where speed is essential."

By Claudia Cavaliere, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

EU Readies Action Against Italy Over Golden Power in UniCredit-BPM Case
data.description:
(Alliance News) - The dispute between UniCredit and Banco BPM may not be over yet, as reported by Il Corriere della Sera on Friday.

Citing Reuters, the European Commission is preparing to take action against Italian legislation on Golden Power, which it considers an obstacle to banking consolidation in Europe.

Brussels accuses the Meloni government of imposing restrictions that limit competition and violate the independence of banks. According to Il Corriere, two proceedings against Italy are expected by mid-November: one concerning the single market and another regarding mergers.

Already in July, the Commission had criticized the conditions set by Palazzo Chigi on UniCredit's offer for Banco BPM, deeming them incompatible with EU law.

The initiative, the newspaper continues, aims to encourage the creation of stronger European banking groups and also serves as a warning to Germany and Spain, where the Commerzbank and Sabadell cases remain unresolved.

By Claudia Cavaliere, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

data.title: Evolution Wins Legal Battle to Unmask Source Behind Short Seller Report
data.description:
Live casino developer Evolution has emerged victorious in its final legal battle to uncover the identity behind the 2021 short seller report that wiped out a significant portion of its market value.

The Supreme Court of the state of New Jersey, USA, has denied the Israeli firm Black Cube's request for appeal. This means Black Cube must either disclose the name of its secret client or face charges of contempt of court, according to the igaming site Next.io. Black Cube was responsible for compiling the report on behalf of the mysterious client.

Evolution has spent several years pursuing the name behind the report and has won every legal challenge along the way.

“I believe the world will eventually discover the truth, but it may take some time,” Evolution CEO Martin Carlesund told Next.io.

The report was released in November 2021 via the law firm Calcagni & Kanefsky, which acted as the public face of the report. The report accused Evolution of operating illegally in certain prohibited markets and of violating U.S. sanctions. The allegations damaged Evolution's reputation and resulted in tens of billions being wiped from its market capitalization.

Bloomberg News previously reported that a competitor of Evolution may have been responsible for the report, but this has not been confirmed.

Indonesia's waste-to-energy plan for 33 cities may require $5.5 billion in funding, minister says
data.description:
JAKARTA (Reuters) -Indonesia's plan to build waste-to-energy projects in 33 cities across the country may require around 91 trillion rupiah ($5.49 billion) in funding, investment minister Rosan Roeslani told reporters on Friday.

The programme, designed to address a waste crisis in some parts of the country, will be launched in 10 cities next month, Rosan said.

"One area can have several plants," said Rosan, who is also the head of sovereign wealth fund Danantara Indonesia. "We will launch the programme in early November."

The priority cities include Jakarta, where there will be four to five sites, as well as other cities in the Java and Bali regions, Rosan said previously.

Partnering with technology providers, Danantara will invest in the projects, while state utility Perusahaan Listrik Negara will take on the electricity they generate.

($1 = 16,570 rupiah)

(Reporting by Fransiska Nangoy; Writing by Stefanno Sulaiman; Editing by David Stanway)

data.title: Sunny Optical to Swap Shanghai Unit for Nearly 2 Billion Yuan Stake in Goertek Unit; Shares Fall 3%
MPS Focuses on Integration with Mediobanca; Generali Board Meeting Anticipated
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Monte dei Paschi di Siena is concentrating on its integration with Mediobanca, according to a report by Il Corriere della Sera on Friday.

"Banco BPM is an excellent bank, but today we are focused on the project with Piazzetta Cuccia," stated CEO Luigi Lovaglio during a hearing before the Parliamentary Commission of Inquiry into the banking system, emphasizing that "the speed of execution is crucial," Il Corriere continues.

Following the OPAS and the preparation of the board of directors' slate, Lovaglio reiterated that it is still "premature" to discuss a second phase involving a possible delisting or merger between MPS and Mediobanca. The latter could remain independent, focusing on private and investment banking, while retail operations would be managed by MPS, the newspaper adds.

Regarding Generali, of which MPS is the largest shareholder, Lovaglio highlighted the strategic importance of this stake, noting it helps diversify revenues and develop the bancassurance segment.

On Monday, as Il Corriere recalls, a Generali board meeting will be held in Trieste--the first since Mediobanca's stake rose to 13%. The board may discuss the creation of a general manager position, which has been vacant for years, in view of a possible succession plan for CEO Philippe Donnet. Among the candidates is Giulio Terzariol, current CEO Insurance.

The discussion takes place in a delicate context following the MPS-Mediobanca OPAS and the strengthening of Delfin and Caltagirone's positions in Generali, with the aim of moving forward in a coordinated manner.

By Claudia Cavaliere, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

Coloplast Veteran and Interim CEO Lars Rasmussen to Leave Board
data.description:
Danish medical technology company Coloplast's interim CEO, Lars Rasmussen, will step down from the board at the upcoming annual general meeting, according to a press release.

Rasmussen has served as chairman of the company for seven years, but left that position in May to take over as interim CEO following Kristian Villumsen's announced departure. Prior to this, Rasmussen was CEO of Coloplast from 2008 to 2018. In total, he has been with the company for nearly four decades.

"It has been an honor and a privilege to serve this company for most of my life, and I have decided to retire from my board duties at a time when the company is well positioned for the future," said 66-year-old Lars Rasmussen.

He will continue as interim CEO until a permanent replacement is found.

The annual general meeting will be held on December 4, 2025. At that time, Niels Christiansen will be proposed as a new board member, and it is expected that he will assume the role of chairman no later than at the following annual general meeting in December 2026. Jette Nygaard-Andersen is currently serving as interim chair.

WeWork India flat in pre-open trading debut
data.description:
(Reuters) -Shares of WeWork India were largely flat in pre-open trading on Friday after a muted response to its initial public offering, as investor caution carried into debut day amid concerns over its steep valuation and governance issues.

The stock listed at 650 rupees on the National Stock Exchange, marginally up from its offer price of 648 rupees.

($1 = 88.7770 Indian rupees)

(Reporting by Meenakshi Maidas and Yagnoseni Das in Bengaluru; Editing by Sonia Cheema)

Vietnam domestic market commodity prices - October 10
EMEA Morning Briefing : Markets Wrap Up Another Week of Uncertainty
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

No major economic or corporate updates expected.

Opening Call:

European stock futures were flat after Asia stocks traded mixed. The dollar weakened, U.S. Treasurys were flat. Gold rose and oil fell.

Equities:

European futures were mixed as markets took a breather with investors taking profits and riskier assets pulling back from the sharp rallies of recent weeks alongside demand for safe-haven assets amid the U.S. government shutdown.

The shutdown, now well into its second week, has started to disrupt businesses, with some companies facing payment delays and considering layoffs.

It is also delaying crucial economic data releases, including September's jobs report, that could affect the Federal Reserve's monetary policy. Alternative jobs data from Wall Street firms have indicated a cooling U.S. labor market.

"When labor markets start to get weak, that does cause equity drawdowns once it reaches a certain point," said Matt Stucky, chief portfolio manager of equities at Northwestern Mutual Wealth Management. "I don't think we're there yet, but that's a risk factor."

Forex:

The U.S. dollar weakened, as financial markets appear to have taken a pause, though the ongoing U.S. government shutdown is still unresolved, says OCBC's global markets research team.

This is delaying key economic releases and contributing to near-term uncertainty, the team said.

Bonds:

The yield on U.S. Treasurys were flat. Despite the lack of data releases, traders are viewing an October rate cut as a virtual lock.

Federal Reserve governor Michael Barr urged caution with further rate moves in a Thursday speech, noting that he isn't satisfied with the central bank's progress in bringing inflation back down to the 2% target.

"The attitude of investors is so positive right now that the burden of proof is falling on the bears to figure out why not to go up, and that's why we've seen what we've seen over the last couple of weeks," said Mark Hackett, chief market strategist at Nationwide.

Energy:

Oil edged fell amid potential supply disruption risks. The U.S. has unveiled more sanctions on Iran, ANZ Research analysts said.

"The Treasury Department sanctioned more than 50 individuals, entities and vessels that facilitate Iranian oil and liquified petroleum gas sales and shipments from the country," the analysts said.

Metals:

Gold continued to rise with strong catalysts likely to continue to support prices, which have powered past the $4,000 mark, shrugging off dollar strength, said Alexandra Symeonidi, corporate credit analyst at William Blair.

Investor and central bank demand has remained resilient despite added costs to further accumulate gold, she said, noting that central banks returned to buying gold in August after a brief pause in July.

However, despite strong gold prices, platinum remains the best-performing metal across precious and base metals, with gains of over 80% year to date, while silver crossed $50 for the first time in decades.

-

Copper is lower due to profit-taking. Copper prices may face short-term corrections, but the medium-to-long-term outlook remains bullish, said Nanhua Futures' analysts say in a commentary.

-

Iron ore rose with markets subdued given the recent Chinese holidays.

Markets will likely be concerned about any further efforts to limit China's steel exports, said ANZ Research strategists Daniel Hynes and Soni Kumari.

China accounts for about 37% of Europe's steel imports, and with European officials reportedly considering doubling tariffs on certain imports to 50%, combined with Beijing's crackdown on overcapacity, a reduction in output is expected.

Given weak domestic demand, "any further constraints to steel exports could lead to weakness in demand for steelmaking raw materials such as iron ore," they said.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Stocks, Gold Pull Back as Shutdown Uncertainty Persists

Markets took a breather on Thursday, with investors taking profits and riskier assets pulling back after the sharp rallies of recent weeks.

The S&P 500 declined 0.3% and the tech-focused Nasdaq composite retreated 0.1% after both indexes closed at all-time highs on Wednesday. The Dow Jones Industrial Average lost 0.5%, or 243 points.

China's Rare-Earth Escalation Threatens Trade Talks-and the Global Economy

China's newest restrictions on rare-earth materials would mark a nearly unprecedented export control that stands to disrupt the global economy, giving Beijing more leverage in trade negotiations and ratcheting up pressure on the Trump administration to respond.

The rule, put out Thursday by China's Commerce Ministry, is viewed as an escalation in the U.S.-China trade fight because it threatens the supply chain for semiconductors. Chips are the lifeblood of the economy, powering phones, computers and data centers needed to train artificial-intelligence models. The rule also would affect cars, solar panels and the equipment for making chips and other products, limiting the ability of other countries to support their own industries. China produces roughly 90% of the world's rare-earth materials.

Fed's Barr Urges Caution With Further Rate Moves, Underscoring Committee's Divisions

After the Federal Reserve's initial rate cut last month, the central bank should proceed carefully with further adjustments to its policy stance, Fed governor Michael Barr said, citing concerns that prices are still rising too quickly.

Barr said Thursday that he remains worried that inflation could persist, and argued that the Fed can't be complacent about bringing inflation's pace back to the central bank's 2% target. The comments suggest Barr may be skeptical of supporting the interest-rate cuts that investors widely expect from the Fed over the rest of 2025, adding a wrinkle to the voting math on a divided policy committee.

How Trump's Upside-Down Diplomacy Delivered a Major Foreign-Policy Victory

President Trump's announcement that he ended the two-year war in Gaza rested on an unorthodox strategy of declaring victory first and forcing others to fill in the details to make it a reality.

He turned upside-down the traditional playbook for solving international crises, in which diplomats work behind the scenes to iron out differences between warring parties, before world leaders swoop in and announce a deal.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com
